description: A maritime garden blending Chinese and Western styles, featuring a Piano Museum and beautiful scenery.
A well-preserved coastal defense fortress from the Qing Dynasty's Self-Strengthening Movement, offering panoramic sea views.
The site of the Zheng Chenggong Statue, a seaside park with scenic views and a memorial to a national hero.
One of the most beautiful university campuses in China, seamlessly combining red-brick Jiageng architecture with modern campus design.
A burgeoning cultural and creative hub in Xiamen, home to art studios, cafes, and trendy shops.
A natural beach favored by Xiamen locals, featuring fine sand, clear waters, and a relatively tranquil atmosphere.
The mausoleum of Mr. Tan Kah Kee, a cultural landscape park integrating commemoration, gardens, and sea views.
Xiamen's seaside avenue, acclaimed as the world's most beautiful marathon route and a top choice for cycling with ocean views.
Xiamen's most picturesque coastline, a paradise for cycling amidst coconut breezes and a popular photography spot.
Xiamen's most popular urban beach, ideal for enjoying sea views, sunsets, and relaxation.
Xiamen's aerial sightseeing cableway, connecting Wanshi Botanical Garden and Zhonggu Mountain, offering panoramic city views.
A modern seaside resort area with well-equipped beach facilities and expansive, magnificent ocean views.
The main commercial street on Gulangyu Island, featuring a variety of Minnan-style snacks and souvenir shops.
Xiamen's busiest commercial street, known for its arcade-style architecture and concentration of shopping and dining options.
The city's green lung, an ecological park for birdwatching and flower viewing, with views of the cross-sea bridge.
The highest peak on Gulangyu Island and Xiamen's best observation point, offering a 360-degree panoramic view of the city.
An artistic district transformed from an original fishing village, filled with small shops and a strong artistic vibe.
